data.narrative:
Black Forest Labs, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, and Patrick Esser, has raised $300M to date and carries a stated valuation exceeding $1B. The relevant peer set comprises five same-stage AI & Software companies, with valuations ranging from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence), alongside one public comparable (PLTR).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using total capital raised ($300M) as a proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $570M. Peer-median analysis across the five-company cohort produces $5.0B, reflecting the sector's wide dispersion. The peer range itself ($500M to $32.0B) illustrates material uncertainty in how frontier-stage AI firms command market value.

Triangulation across both methods supports a range of $484M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.8B. The stated valuation exceeding $1B sits within this envelope but below the peer median, consistent with Black Forest Labs' position among a heterogeneous cohort. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set—particularly whether Safe Superintelligence's $32.0B valuation reflects durable market consensus or outlier pricing—and the limited applicability of stage-progression methodology to a capital-efficient, high-burn-rate sector where funding size correlates imperfectly with value creation.
